violations.text: The flip top prep unit at the cookline was observed to be in poor repair (observed maintaining ambient temperature of 48 degrees Fahrenheit). Unit was repaired on 6/24/25. PIC contacted maintenance for the unit to be serviced again. Unit to not be used to store any (TCS foods) time/temperature controlled foods for safety. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

An employee was observed washing their hands at the 3-bay sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. Hand-washing procedures reviewed with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Beef was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit in the flip top prep unit. The prep unit was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, foods were placed in the unit 2 hours prior to inspection. Foods relocated to walk-in cooler to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The establishment could not provide records of using Time as a Public Health Control for sushi rice. TPHC logs must be kept for 90 days and updated daily. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Foods were observed in containers greater than four inches deep on the counter after cooking. Foods moved to sheet pans to properly cool before being placed in the refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Reduced oxygen packaged crab meat and mussels labeled "KEEP FROZEN" were observed thawing with the package intact. ROP seafood labeled "KEEP FROZEN" must be removed from the packaging upon thawing. Person in charge removed foods from packaging to allow for oxygen transfer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The following was observed: 1) The gaskets of the walk-in freezer and the refrigeration units at the cookline was observed in poor repair (frayed). 2) The flip top prep unit at the cookline was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 46 degrees Fahrenheit New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed unclean: 1) The hood ventilation system was observed with an accumulation of grease 2) The wires above the prep units were observed with dust New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Prime rib was observed held at 90 degrees Fahrenheit at the front prep area. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC elected to dispose of food at the time of inspection. A new piece was made and kept in the hot-holding unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Pre-packaged mousse/pudding labeled "keep refrigerated" was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit in the dessert prep unit. Per PIC, the pudding was placed in the unit 2 hours prior to inspection. Items were moved to the freezer to rapidly cool. Per PIC, items will be held in the freezer on the buffet line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Noodles were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and covered 1 hour prior to inspection. Noodles were moved to sheet pans to rapidly cool. PIC given cooling methods guide and cooling logs for review at next inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024
Scour pads were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. At the time of inspection, dish washer was serviced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

Nime Chow was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit at the buffet line. Per PIC, food was put on the line 2 hours prior to inspection. Food moved to walk-in cooler to cool rapidly. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

Chicken was observed held at 70 degrees Fahrenheit at the wok-line. Per PIC, chicken was made 2 hours prior to inspection and left at the line until the menu change for dinner. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken was reheated above 165 at the time of inspection. Pre-packaged mousse/pudding labeled "keep refrigerated" was observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit in the dessert prep unit. Per PIC, the pudding was placed in the unit 4 hours prior to inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of items at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Noodles were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep 2 hours prior to inspection. Noodles were moved to sheet pans to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

Sushi display unit located in the buffet area was not in good repair(inadequate ambient temperature).PIC removed food product to colder refrigeration unit Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The following physical facilities have an accumulation of debris: 1.floors in walk in coolers observed with soil and food residue and other debris. 2.fans in walk cooler observed with dust 3.ice buildup was observed on fans and pipes in walk in freezer in basement. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
The ceilings in kitchen and basement area is in poor repair(observed holes). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/23/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine and knives) were observed with an accumulation of food debris or biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch.PIC voluntarily disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2023

Utensils in the kitchen/warewashing area are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2023
Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2023

The floors throughout the facility and walk in coolers have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2023
